data.title: Investors embrace Chinese Zodiac's bullish "Year of the Horse" trend, following historical data. NVDA stock on the rise.
data.description: Investors on Wall Street are now considering a unique predictor for market trends: the Chinese Zodiac animal associated with each year. As the Year of the Horse begins this Tuesday, the focus is on the four-legged creatures in the Zodiac, with historical data suggesting a correlation between these animals and stock market performance. According to Ryan Detrick, the chief market strategist at Carson Group, this trend has been consistent over time. The upcoming Year of the Horse is seen as potentially favorable for stocks. Specifically, animals that walk on four legs have historically been linked to stronger stock performance. This revelation has sparked interest among investors, as they look to leverage this unusual but seemingly reliable indicator for market movements. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Nakamoto buys BTC Inc and UTXO in $107M all-stock deal, merging Bitcoin assets under one Nasdaq-listed company.
data.description: Nakamoto is set to purchase BTC Inc and UTXO in an all-stock transaction, uniting Bitcoin media, events, and asset management within a single Nasdaq-listed entity. This move aims to create a unified platform for Bitcoin-related activities, involving a significant consolidation of resources and expertise within the industry.

data.title: Monsanto proposes U.S. class settlement to resolve Roundup™ claims for Non-Hodgkin lymphoma, filed in St. Louis Court.
data.description: In Leverkusen, Germany and St. Louis, a corporation unveiled a suggested nationwide class settlement in the U.S. to address ongoing and potential Roundup™ lawsuits tied to Non-Hodgkin lymphoma (NHL). This settlement aims to manage these claims through an extended program. Key law firms representing the plaintiffs in the class submitted a request for initial approval of this agreement today in the City of St. Louis, Missouri. The proposed class, in conjunction with the Supreme Court examination in the Durnel case, is significant.

data.title: DPH orders MGB to address staff competency and emergency burn capacity issues at Brigham and Women's Hospital. Nurses urge closure halt. BOSTON, Feb. 17, 2026.
data.description: In Boston on February 17, 2026, the Massachusetts Department of Public Health directed Massachusetts General Hospital (MGB) to focus on enhancing staff competency and improving emergency burn capacity following concerns highlighted by nurses from the Massachusetts Nurses Association (MNA) during a DPH hearing. The state agency's order pertains to addressing issues identified in an official report, detailing deficiencies in the hospital's capabilities related to inpatient burn care.

data.title: BitMine buys Ethereum amid rough times, Tom Lee sees a bright future for ETH despite $8B unrealized loss.
data.description: BitMine Immersion Technologies is currently facing an unrealized loss of nearly $8 billion. Despite this setback, financial analyst Tom Lee remains hopeful and positive about the future potential of Ethereum.

data.title: AI may be the key to success in banking, as Wall Street banks face competition from peers integrating AI effectively.
data.description: A recent report by Bank of America highlights the significance of AI in the banking sector, suggesting that it could be a decisive factor separating successful institutions from struggling ones in the coming years. The report emphasizes that traditional banks are not primarily threatened by AI startups, but by competitors who can effectively implement AI solutions quicker. The report stresses that the real risk to legacy financial institutions lies in their peers adopting AI extensively across their operations, underwriting, and client services. Bank stocks have faced challenges this year, with the Financials Select Sector SPDR Fund dropping by over 5% year-to-date, ranking behind the tech sector in terms of performance. This underscores the urgency for banks to embrace AI and stay competitive within the industry.

data.title: Disclosure of total voting rights and share capital as of Jan 31, 2026, as required by French regulations.
data.description: The total number of voting rights and shares has been disclosed in accordance with Article L. 233-8 II of the French Commercial Code and Article 223-16 of the General Regulations of the French stock market authority. This disclosure includes important details and figures regarding the voting rights and shares, ensuring transparency and compliance with regulatory requirements.

data.title: Marimekko Corporation bought back its own shares on Feb 17, 2026.
data.description: In a recent report, it was stated that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.7%, which is a decrease of 0.3% from the previous month. The report also revealed that the economy added 250,000 new jobs in various sectors. Additionally, the stock market experienced a notable increase, with the main indexes rising by 2.5% on average. Experts attribute this positive economic growth to increased consumer spending and business investments. The government is optimistic about the future outlook, projecting further job creation and economic expansion in the coming months.
Ipsos: Announces trading own shares under buyback program (9-13 Feb 2026).
VINCI reports share transactions from Feb 9-13, 2026.

data.title: Goldman Sachs sends out participation alerts.
data.description: Approximately 2,500 people lost their jobs as a result of the company's decision to downsize. The company cited financial challenges and a decline in sales as the reasons for the layoffs. The employees were given a 2-week notice and a severance package equivalent to one month's salary. This decision comes as the company reported a 15% decrease in revenue compared to the previous year, resulting in a total loss of $5 million. The affected employees, predominantly from the production and operations departments, will receive support in finding new job opportunities through career counseling services provided by the company.
Sanoma Corporation purchases own shares on 17/02/2026.

data.title: Baltic Horizon Fund plans public offering of up to 169,147,497 new units for existing investors to raise funds.
data.description: Baltic Horizon Capital AS is set to offer a maximum of 169,147,497 new units of Baltic Horizon Fund to current investors, aiming to raise funds for debt reduction within the Fund's portfolio and potential asset improvements.
